patterns_recognized: OWNER BvP concentration (>=2 batters, >=14 PA, shrunk_OPS >0.800) overcame Paul Skenes' elite F1_xera_gap=4.25 on 5/28 (CHC won 7-2); >=2 OWNER-level hitters vs any SP warrants at least one tier of directional caution regardless of xERA magnitude.

D-grade dual-signal-against (SP xERA AND PQI both opposing pick) validated 3-for-3 on 5/28 blowouts (DET 7-1, BOS 10-2, TEX 5-1); archive cumulative ~60% directional — treat as moderate DOWNGRADE trigger when both oppose the pick.

Large f5_full_delta (>=0.12) with favorable hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap (negative = pick side pen better) resolves as bullpen-carry WIN, not directional reversal: 5/26 NYY@KC 15-1 and 5/28 MIN@CHW 6-2 both confirmed. Only treat large f5_full_delta as a reversal concern when hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap is positive.

HOU@TEX series 0/3 over three consecutive games (5/25, 5/26, 5/28); series-level structural volatility overrides SP and F-signal conviction in both directions — not directly relevant today but pattern on file for any HOU@TEX appearance.
